item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How heavy are they — well, the density of animal tissue is on a level with that of water. Some tissues are much denser — e.g., mineralaceous infrastructures like bone or carapace — while others are rather less so — e.g., adipose and neural tissues. So let's just call it 'water density' all the way through.

The density of water — and thus, barnacle tissue — is 997 kg/m³.

What's the volume of a barnacle shape that's "on average, 1 meter tall and 10 meters in circumference at the base"? A barnacle can be approximated as a truncated cone, and based on my admittedly vague memories of seeing barnacles in the wild, and the pictures of barnacles that I'm seeing on Wikimedia Commons, I'm going to estimate that the area of the top is from 1/3 to 1/2 that of the base. Split the difference and say A of top is 5/12 of base. And since these are fictitious anyway, let's say I'm exactly right.

Circumference of base is 10 m, which is 2 pi R, which gives R of about 1.6, and thus A of base is about 8 m2, of which 5/12 is 3 1/3 m2.

A is pi r2. Thus, r of top is about 1.025.

Formula for volume of a truncated cone is:

1/3 times pi times height times (r2 + r*R + R2)

=

1/3 * pi * 1m * ( (1.025)2 + (1.025)(1.6) + (1.6)2)

=

about 5.5 m3

times the aforementioned 997 kg / m3

equals just under five and a half metric tons.

Since you asked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How heavy are SCP-2714-1 and are they bonded to the floor like normal barnacles? Can they even be moved?

SCP-2714 has two pages that display separate anomalous properties. Opening the page 20-21 spread will cause the appearance of three sessile organisms resembling barnacles, designated SCP-2714-1, in a varying distance around SCP-2714-1.

I think you mean "around SCP-2714"

(Foundation agents embedded at the gallery event observed Michael Powell, noted member of Are We Cool Yet? whose work was being presented, sitting at the gallery bar with Escoffier for three hours. Although the record of the conversation was recorded, it was destroyed for security reasons related to the surveillance.)

"record of the conversation was recorded" sounds clunky. I'd recommend deleting or changing one of the words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Billions of blue blistering barnacles. Glad to see Tintin get some love!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Rastapopalous fires at the spacecraft.)

In the comic, Allan fires at the spacecraft.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This article has two easy spots for inline links from what I can see. The first is the mention of Site-88, which can be turned into a link to the Site-88 Dossier. The second is the mention of AWCY? which can link to the GoI's hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2714 is to be kept in a low-value containment safe at Site 88. On no account are pages 20 and 21 to be opened outside of occasional approved testing. Pages 57 and 58 are to be opened and the contents recorded daily. A standard-issue containment bookmark has been slotted into these pages to avoid opening pages 20 and 21 during the procedure. All documents related to SCP-2714 are to be stored in low-level object supplement folders next to the safe for researcher reference.

To this:

SCP-2714 is to be kept in a low-value containment safe at Site-88. On no account are pages 20 and 21 to be opened outside of occasional approved testing. Pages 57 and 58 are to be opened and the contents recorded daily. A standard-issue containment bookmark has been slotted into these pages to avoid opening pages 20 and 21 during the procedure. All documents related to SCP-2714 are to be stored in low-level object supplement folders next to the safe for researcher reference.

Changing this:

Also met with some strange men at the gallery. Seemed to me they were especially interested in my father. One of them, Australian accented, said he was named Powell and he was interested in commissioning a piece for a few of father's men. Some diorama sculpture to fill one of their corporate lobbies. Powell and another man, Tanhauser, they gave me a card. "ARE WE COOL YET?" and then a phone number and email address.

To this:

Also met with some strange men at the gallery. Seemed to me they were especially interested in my father. One of them, Australian accented, said he was named Powell and he was interested in commissioning a piece for a few of father's men. Some diorama sculpture to fill one of their corporate lobbies. Powell and another man, Tanhauser, they gave me a card. "ARE WE COOL YET?" and then a phone number and email address.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how in the first paragraph of the item description you completely throw the old item, lots of wear Foundation cliche out of the window. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a big fan of Tintin, I love you for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Updated and Crosslinked to the Sarkic Hub as part of the WikiWalk

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats this hit +50!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never read Tintin before this article, but did read 714 and some other issues to better appreciate it, so if you wanted to accomplish this, op, then good job.

Here's what I'm reading into it.

A common theme in rituals is the sacrifice. Sacrifice needs value. Cut the heart out of your firstborn. Make a knife out of your family silver. Burn the best sheep of the flock. The only photograph of your mother. Prove your intent. Let go.

translated from French into English by Leslie Lonsdale-Cooper and Michael Turner and published by Methuen Children's Books. SCP-2714 shows signs of its age, with some minor damage, but is in mostly good condition.

Collector and fanatic didn't feel like ruining his autographed first print original, so he bought a spare "good condition" from ebay.

If you want to make gods, and you perform a fake ritual, what you get is fake gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this once before, and I must have passed it by without comment. The barnacles confused me until I read the author's note here, but despite having to have half the anomaly explained to me, the strength of the story and characters really makes this work, so have a late upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that you both got it. It's both, actually. There's essentially two stories going on here. An outer one, and an internal struggle. There was hubris from all parties, and it all resulted in the same tragedy.

Escoffier is probably the most relatable Sarkicist in many ways. I feel like we can all understand him on some level. We've all been to that kind of place in our lives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I've realized — and discussed this with LordStonefish on IRC — that AWCY isn't even necessary here.

Interesting input overall, although I would think that AWCY does have a place here. I mean, the most common depiction of Sarkicism is that they are this nigh-apocalyptic cult that will has trashed the ancient world and will trash the modern world. To see their gods screwed over by anomalous art shows that they are not the be-all and end-all of the Foundation-verse's fate.

But heck, I am exploiting applicability and headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I've realized — and discussed this with LordStonefish on IRC — that AWCY isn't even necessary here.

The barnacles are indicative of why. They allude to the phrase "billions of blistering blue barnacles", which only appeared in the English translation. Escoffier was a francophone. Why would he have the English-language version instead of the French-language original?

He was a collector and a completionist. This is why he had the translated version — but also, it's why he used the translated version. He didn't want to use his prized original! It was a first printing! It was even autographed! If he sacrificed it in the ritual, it'd be ruined!

So instead of sacrificing to his gods something he truly valued and prized and cherished, he used something which he considered a barely-adequate substitute. Maybe he figured the gods wouldn't notice?

And now his gods are lobotomized blobs of flesh based on the English dialogue.

If you look at the original French, Capitaine Haddock's original swearing — the phrase which was translated to "billions of blistering blue barnacles" — was "mille millions de mille sabords de tonnerre de Brest". That basically means he was swearing by gunports from which cannons were firing. Imagine what would have happened if that had formed the basis of the godbodies.

The godminds aren't trapped in the book forever, though. Just until the original summoning is complete. They just have to form "billions of blistering blue barnacles", then they'll finally be free.

Literally, they have to form billions of barnacloid godbodies. A minimum of two billion. 2 * 109.

And they can only form three at a time. And each triad takes ten minutes to form. And they can only form when the book is open to the right page.

The book would have to be open to that page for over eleven thousand years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What shout outs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Comes from the Sarkic tag list

Reads first sentance of the description

Wait, WHAT?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically, you tell Escoffier's entire life story and build him into a rounded character with complex motivations and characterizations over the course of those few addenda. He had an existance beyond just the creation of this anomaly… but the creation of this anomaly is where that story ended, in a tragic way. That's a strong narrative, there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in chat without the backstory, and being unsure about it there. The backstory is excellently written and comprehensible, and I'm a fan of both Tintin and Sarkicism, so this gets my +1

I am Castafiore, the Milanese Nightingale, and I have borne an intolerable force. For someone came at me headlong in the morning and dismembered me with a bottle of whisky and tore me apart, according to the rigor of harmony. What nonsense!

This line is priceless

Also, why doesn't the Recovery Log have a date?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Standard-issue containment bookmark.

I love it.

Also, you combined two of my favorite things (Tintin and Sarkicism), and you did it well. +1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not the author here, but I did something digging on the matter.

The book cover, as found on Wikipedia, is under fair use. Specifically, its licensing is this:

This image is of book cover(s), and the copyright for it is most likely owned either by the artist who created the cover(s) or the publisher of the book(s). It is believed that the use of low-resolution images of book covers
to illustrate an article discussing the book in question
on the English-language Wikipedia, hosted on servers in the United States by the non-profit Wikimedia Foundation,
qualifies as fair use under United States copyright law. Other use of this image, on Wikipedia or elsewhere, might be copyright infringement. See Wikipedia:Non-free content for more information.

So yeah, it seems really dodgy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In summary, a Sarkicist got screwed over by anartists. We have gone a long way since the apocalyptic SK-class causing beings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To add:

The reason they're trapped is because the AWCY anartists sabotaged the arcane ritual, sealing the monsters in Escoffier's beloved book. They basically used Escoffier to get to his father, who they intend to kill, usurp his power/replace him as Karcists, and use it to make millions off their art.

So Escoffier could never rejoin the religion, his gods are being tortured for all time, and his father he so desperately wanted to connect with is dead.

And so an innocent Sarkicist, broken by the world, dies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
here's what's going on.

Mr Escoffier was a Tintin fanatic who tried to summon lovecraftian monstrosities, but their minds accidentally got trapped within the Tintin book.

When you open the book to the page in question, the monstrosities' (Tintin-based) bodies instantiate, but remain mindless and inert, because their minds are trapped in the panels of the book. They keep screaming and ranting in Tintin-based vocabularies, and they can't even stop making Tintin allusions.

Their bodies are based on the four main characters of Tintin (three humans and a dog): Tintin (orange hair and light blue outfit), Captain Haddock (black hair and dark blue outfit), Professor Calculus (black hair and green outfit), and Snowy (white dog). The whiskey is because Captain Haddock was a heavy drinker, and they're giant barnacles because one of Captain Haddock's character traits was his ridiculously ornate swearing, including a phrase which was translated into English as "BILLIONS OF BLUE BLISTERING BARNACLES".

If the monstrosity minds could control the bodies, they could wreak great terror on this pathetic world. But they can't. They can't control the bodies, they can't access them, they can't even make the bodies die or de-instantiate. All they can do is alter the text in the speech bubbles on two pages of one copy of one graphic novel.

And then you close the book, and the entities' minds are forced back into dormancy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do any of you think this would look better if there was a picture of the cover of the book in the SCP? It would catch a lot of people's eyes.

Unless you can't do it for copyright reasons which I understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked elements of this. The anart TinTin angle was interesting but by the end I'd lost sight of the bigger picture. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What did you specifically like about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My head canon is they appropriated the brand for their own gains, so you're not far off.

Crouching hipster, hidden Karcist, if you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, it was hard. Some people loved the backstory and the anomaly was boring, some people thought it cluttered it up.

I guess you can't please everyone, and the result is busyness when you try. Oh well.

Thanks for upvoting anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
another man, Tanhauser, they gave me a card. "ARE WE COOL YET?" and then a phone number and email address

Odd, since many depictions of AWCY have them a expression that is definitively decentralised. However, there is no rule to say that someone won't take that "brand" and appropriate it for their purposes. It's not like AWCY have lawyers to take down those types.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy crud do I love this.
Never even opened a Tintin comic, and the anomaly itself isn't anything to write home about… but that backstory. Freaking awesome.
Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I like this enough to upvote, I have to echo what ARD said above. It feels like way too many plot threads going on at once.

Tentative Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it isn't a real barrier, not sure. All I know is that I am reminded that Tintin is a thing that I know less about than I would like to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I add more footnotes explaining things? I thought the underlying story was interesting enough that the barrier could be hurdled if need be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like a lack of familiarity with the source material is a barrier here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Missed some XXXX in the first few paragraphs of the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the thought that counts. Thank you for reading and giving feedback though. Much appreciated! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking as somebody who owns almost all of the Tintins, even Lake of Sharks (with the exception of In the Congo and In the Land of the Soviets), I feel that this started strong but then lost its way in the end. While I understand and enjoy the shoutouts in the biologies of the 2714-1 instances, I feel that you tried to shove too many different plot threads into this. The tone of the journal entries, especially the last one, feels inconsistent.

I genuinely want to like this. Unfortunately, I can't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Special thanks to Feline, Lazar, Metaphysician, Conwell, Jekeled and, indirectly, GreenWolf, Moenennbys and SunnyClockwork for their chat feedback. Special thanks to MrWrong and Kapitano for forum feedback. My very special thanks go out to Voct because without him this story would not be in the shape it is and to that I am greatly thankful
